transcript.text: Hello. I've been in the habit of uploading like a lot of videos lately so I figure I'll just keep it up and I've got another kind of video series that I think I might start doing and this is the dreams that I have.

And my mom would freak out. A lot of people would say, oh, I'll talk about your dreams. That's exposing too much of yourself. But I think it's a cool thing to expose a lot of yourself. That's kind of, I think, what a lot of people should be doing these days.

And if I do it myself, maybe I can motivate other people to do it and then people share and then we can find out more about each other. So, now I haven't studied dream interpretation, but I think I can do it. You know, you just trust yourself.

So, anyways, I had a dream. Last night, I went mountain biking with some people, some friends, like on a riverbed. And then we'd go down this riverbed and then we went to like the border between America and Mexico.

And there was this fence and the fence had this big gaping hole, like somebody had torn a hole into it. And I remember we were thinking like, huh, well, nobody knows how easy it is to get from America to Mexico. It's kind of interesting.
So, maybe the meaning of that was that if you follow a path and you want to do something, all you have to do is follow the path and then you can do it and there's really no obstacle.

Anyways, and then we start riding our bikes back and it was this beautiful trail, like lots of people on a dirt trail and I was on a mountain bike and my friend rides ahead of me and we're only going like 10 miles an hour and I'm riding as hard, as hard as I can.
I'm only going 10 miles an hour. And so I'm like, and I'm so quick and my friend's like, you know, what's going on? What's wrong?
And I look and then we stop and I look at my bike and I had a, uh, like a rock or something stuck between the tire and the frame, which was keeping me from going very fast.
And I was pedaling so hard that I actually took all the rubber off and like the mountain, the knobby part of the mountain bike tire was all like worn away and it was just flat.
And they said, Oh wow. Well, that's what's wrong. And, uh, and, um, so I think the meaning of that dream was that, well, I've had dreams.
I used to have dreams like that all the time when I was a kid where I'm going as fast as I can, running as fast as I can.
I'm only going like one mile an hour. And I think maybe that comes from, uh, I've been kind of sick lately and haven't been able to, you know, exercise.
And I've kind of, it was, it happened right when I got really excited about doing the class of exercises.
So maybe that was kind of like my psychology, like wanting to exercise, but not being able to, and then maybe going out and trying, but just not having the energy.
So maybe that's what it was. So maybe it was just my subconscious being a little bit frustrated by that.
I don't know, but, um, but that I do have motivation. So maybe that's what that was.
And, uh, I had another part of a weird dream last night. I don't really want to talk about that because I don't really understand that.
So anyways, there's my dream journal on video number one. We'll see if I keep it up.
